shell.title: Your care journey
shell.subtitle: Guided support, next steps, and practical help in one calmer place

home.title: Start from what matters most to you right now.
home.description: Find a practitioner, review your sessions, or continue your care journey without the heavy dashboard feeling.

panel.title: A simpler patient flow
panel.description: This area is designed around the real patient journey: discover the right practitioner first, then manage sessions and follow-up in one place.
panel.discoveryValue: Browse practitioners and specialties before dealing with account details or workspace tools.
panel.followupValue: Sessions, messages, and payments stay nearby when you need them, without turning the experience into an admin dashboard.

practitioners.title: Browse practitioners
practitioners.description: Explore available practitioners and start from the public discovery experience.

sessions.title: My sessions
sessions.description: Check upcoming appointments and the next steps in your care journey.

chat.title: Messages
chat.description: Stay close to approved care conversations and future follow-up messages.

profile.title: Profile
profile.description: Keep your account details and personal information easy to reach.

support.title: Keep practical tasks within reach
support.description: Payments, settings, and profile tools still exist, but they support the care journey instead of becoming the main experience.

meta.title: Sawiyaa | Patient Care Space
meta.description: A simple care-first entry point for patients to discover practitioners and follow their sessions.

pendingPayment.label: Action needed
pendingPayment.note: Your booking is reserved — complete the payment to confirm your session.

failedPayment.label: Payment issue
failedPayment.note: One of your recent payments could not be processed.

empty.heading: Start your care journey
empty.note: Find the right practitioner and book your first session.

sessions.title: Keep all your sessions and next steps easy to follow.
sessions.description: This page is where upcoming appointments, past sessions, and care follow-up will become easiest to review.

first.title: Start from your booked care
first.description: Your next appointment and recent sessions will appear here in a simple timeline.

second.title: Know what to do next
second.description: Each session will guide you toward confirmation, joining, follow-up, or any action you still need.

third.title: Stay connected to the journey
third.description: Messages, payments, and future care updates can stay linked to each session instead of being scattered.

tips.first: If you are just getting started, begin by finding the practitioner who feels right for you.
tips.second: Come back here whenever you want a clear view of what is upcoming and what already happened.
tips.third: You should never need to guess the next step after booking.

practitioners.title: Browse practitioners from a patient-first starting point.
practitioners.description: Use the same trusted discovery flow, but within your care area so choosing the right practitioner feels like part of your journey, not a separate tool.

first.title: Start with what matters to you
first.description: Use search and filters to narrow the list based on specialty, language, and the kind of support you are looking for.

second.title: Compare with less effort
second.description: Each practitioner card should help you understand enough to decide whether you want to learn more.

third.title: Move forward when you feel ready
third.description: Once someone feels right, continue into their profile and the next booking steps without leaving the patient journey.

tips.first: You do not need to know the exact practitioner name to begin. Start with specialty or language if that is easier.
tips.second: If the list feels broad, use one or two filters first instead of trying to decide from everything at once.
tips.third: This page should help you choose with confidence, not pressure you into fast decisions.

payments.title: Make payment details easy to read and easy to trust.
payments.description: This page will show session-related charges, payment status, and any billing details in a clearer patient-friendly way.

first.title: See what each payment is for
first.description: Payments should connect directly to the relevant session so the reason behind each charge stays obvious.

second.title: Check status without confusion
second.description: Paid, pending, or failed states should stay visible in plain language rather than technical finance wording.

third.title: Return to care quickly
third.description: After checking a payment, it should stay easy to go back to your session or practitioner without getting lost.

tips.first: Most patients care about clarity, not accounting language, so this page keeps the explanation simple.
tips.second: If a payment is linked to a session, the session context should remain nearby.
tips.third: Any future invoice or receipt flow should stay attached to the session journey.

chat.title: Keep follow-up messages calm, clear, and close to your care journey.
chat.description: This page is for approved conversations and care-related follow-up without making the patient think in workspace or admin terms.

first.title: See the right conversation quickly
first.description: Messages should be grouped in a way that makes the care context obvious at a glance.

second.title: Stay focused on care, not tooling
second.description: The experience should feel like simple communication support around sessions and follow-up.

third.title: Move easily between message and session
third.description: Patients should be able to understand which session or practitioner the conversation belongs to.

tips.first: Conversations should answer patient questions clearly and reduce uncertainty.
tips.second: Messaging should feel supportive, not like a back-office inbox.
tips.third: Context labels and timestamps should do most of the thinking for the user.

settings.title: Keep account settings simple and low-stress.
settings.description: Patients should find the few account controls they need without feeling like they entered an operations panel.

first.title: Focus on the essentials
first.description: Language, account preferences, and security options should stay visible without overload.

second.title: Make each option easy to understand
second.description: Every setting should explain what it changes and why the patient might care.

third.title: Keep important actions safe
third.description: Sensitive account actions should be clear, deliberate, and never hidden behind confusing wording.

tips.first: Patients usually want reassurance more than configuration depth.
tips.second: Short helper text matters here because it removes hesitation.
tips.third: Profile and settings should feel related, not like two unrelated tools.

articles.title: Turn educational content into something supportive and easy to discover.
articles.description: This area should help patients find practical, reassuring content that supports their care journey without distracting them from booking or sessions.

first.title: Start with relevant guidance
first.description: Patients should quickly see content that feels useful to their current stage, not an overwhelming library.

second.title: Keep reading connected to care
second.description: Educational material should support confidence before or after sessions, not compete with them.

third.title: Make returning easy
third.description: Patients should move smoothly from content back to finding a practitioner or reviewing their sessions.

tips.first: The tone should stay calm and supportive rather than overly clinical.
tips.second: Helpful recommendations matter more than deep navigation structures here.
tips.third: This page should reduce uncertainty, not create more choices than necessary.

reviews.title: Make feedback feel simple, useful, and connected to real care moments.
reviews.description: Patients should understand when they can leave feedback and why it matters, without needing to think about systems or moderation flows.

first.title: Connect feedback to completed care
first.description: Reviews should be shown around finished sessions so the context stays obvious.

second.title: Keep the action lightweight
second.description: Patients should feel comfortable sharing their experience without a heavy process.

third.title: Show what was already submitted
third.description: It should be easy to understand which sessions already have feedback and which still do not.

tips.first: Patients are more likely to leave feedback when the timing feels natural.
tips.second: The interface should explain expectations in plain language.
tips.third: Feedback tools should feel like part of the care journey, not a separate admin task.

profile.helper: Keep your basic information up to date so your care journey, communication, and future booking flows stay clearer and more reliable.

first.title: Review the essentials first
first.description: Start with your displayed name, language, and personal details that affect how the platform communicates with you.

second.title: Update only what matters
second.description: The form should stay simple and focused so you do not need to think through unnecessary fields.

third.title: Save with confidence
third.description: Feedback messages should make it obvious whether your information was updated successfully.

tips.first: Keeping your profile current helps future care and support flows feel smoother.
tips.second: Profile details should always stay understandable without technical language.
tips.third: This page should feel personal and supportive, not administrative.
